mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation erodes the real return on nominal bonds, prompting investors to demand higher nominal yields to compensate, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield upward over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day yield change is negative in more than 50% of months when CPI month-over-month growth is positive over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate signals economic weakness, triggering flight-to-safety into Treasuries and compressing yields, so the 10-year yield declines over the next 20 trading days after the rise.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: If the 20-day yield increases in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth indicates robust consumer demand, which lifts GDP growth expectations and raises the term premium, pushing the 10-year yield higher over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 60-day yield change is negative in more than 60% of months over a 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is above 4.5%, the market anticipates future rate cuts, leading to a decline in short-term rate expectations and a flattening of the yield curve that pushes the 10-year yield lower over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day yield increases in more than 50% of triggered observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When both inflation is elevated and the fed funds rate is high, real rates remain constrained, and the market expects persistent policy tightening, which elevates term premiums and drives the 10-year yield upward over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the joint signal (both proxies elevated) produces a yield decline in more than half of observations over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where the unemployment rate is below 4%, the labor market is extremely tight, leading to upward wage pressure and higher inflation expectations, which push the 10-year yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day yield declines in more than 50% of months when UNRATE is below 4% over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with robust GDP expansion indicates broad economic strength, which raises real rate expectations and leads to higher 10-year yields over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 60-day yield change is negative in more than 50% of quarters when both series are above their 12-month moving averages.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal future residential investment and economic momentum, which lifts growth expectations and the natural rate, causing the 10-year yield to rise over the next 5 trading days as the market prices in the positive outlook.
failure_condition: If the 5-day yield change is negatively correlated with monthly housing starts growth over any 5-year rolling window.
